topic: Houston heat — driving without working AC

turns.content: bro october in houston and its still 95 degrees wallahi im dying out here
Houston in October can be brutal — the heat just refuses to quit some years. Are you stuck driving in it all day?
yeah and my ac is barely working. it blows air but its not cold anymore. just warm air like a hair dryer

Oh man, that's rough. Driving all day in Houston heat without cold AC is miserable. It sounds like it might be low on refrigerant, or the compressor could be going out. Have you had anyone look at it yet?
nah i keep putting it off because i know its gonna be expensive. how much does that stuff usually cost

If it's just a refrigerant recharge, you're looking at maybe $150-$250 at a shop. If the compressor is actually failing, that's a bigger job — usually $500-$900 depending on the car and the shop. Some places will do a diagnostic for free or cheap to figure out which one it is before committing to anything.
man i cant afford the compressor right now thats for sure. you think the recharge would fix it at least for a while

It's worth trying. If there's a slow leak, a recharge can buy you a few weeks to a few months of cold air. Some shops even add a leak sealant with the recharge that can help. Also, if you want a cheaper first step, you can buy a DIY recharge kit from AutoZone or Walmart for like $30-$40 and do it yourself — there are tons of YouTube tutorials. That might hold you over.
the diy thing might work. but bro my passengers are complaining too. had a lady yesterday give me 3 stars and said the car was too hot

That's the real problem right there. Bad ratings can hit your earnings and even get you deactivated if they stack up. The AC is basically a business expense at this point — it's protecting your income. Even the $30 DIY recharge is worth doing ASAP just to keep those ratings up.
yeah you right. i been keeping the windows down but some people dont like that either because of the noise on the highway. cant win

Yeah, windows down at highway speed is loud and it messes up people's hair, phone calls, all that. For a temporary fix while you sort the AC, you could try driving with windows cracked and a portable fan clipped to the dash — it's not perfect but it circulates air without the highway noise. But honestly, getting that recharge done this weekend should be priority one.
ima hit autozone tomorrow morning before i start driving. wallahi if this doesnt work i dont know what im gonna do. cant afford a big repair right now

One step at a time. The recharge might be all you need — a lot of the time it's just low refrigerant and nothing more serious. Cross that bridge if you get to it. And hey, November is right around the corner, the heat has to break eventually.
bro in houston november is still 80 degrees lol but yeah at least its not 95. alright ima handle it tomorrow. appreciate you
Ha, fair point — Houston's version of fall is everyone else's summer. Good luck with the recharge tomorrow. Hope it does the trick and gets those ratings back up.
